Alright guys, so now we are going to go into managing your forms. Right, so you might only have one or two forms. As you end up building more and more forms and finding different uses for them, you might end up having quite a few forms and we can use the dashboard to keep them managed and view all of the entries. So this is what your dashboard looks like. You're on the top form here and you can take a look at any of the forms you have and to look at entries like let's say you ran a challenge and you wanted to collect people that signed up for your challenge. You can take a look, you'll hover over the form that you have and you can take a look at a few different things. You can add notifications which will send you a message or an email when a form has been submitted. You can view the form so you can see it exactly as your customers would see it. If you have payments set up that you would do here, share. This is going to be where you can send a link to anybody or embed it. So this would be a great, let's say you want somebody to sign up for an internal challenge that you're running and you want them to fill out a form first so you know how many people you get signed up. This would be a great place to grab that link and post it in your Facebook or somewhere else if you want to embed the form directly into ClickFunnels. This is where you would grab that link. Entries, this is where you can view the entries which we're going to go to in a second and under more, this gives you the API information if you want to integrate it with something else or if you want to create a brand new form but use this one as a template, you would just click on duplicate form. So those are all the options that you have. The final thing is if you want to change the appearance of the form, you can click on this drop down and these are all different appearances. Unfortunately you have to commit to one before it just shows you what it looks like then you can view it and see, hmm, I like that. It looks great or you can leave the original gray one. So what we're going to take a look at is the entries. So we can click on entries and you can click on all entries, especially if you didn't have one done today and it is going to take you to another dashboard where it organizes them by email and it doesn't show a ton of the time. So it will show the first block of them and you can click on any one of these and you can see what somebody filled out. So as you keep clicking, like I said, it doesn't show you a lot at a time but you can search through them which makes it really nice. So if you have a client, maybe Smith, we don't have any Smiths, maybe Jones. All right, well we're just going to go back to the, we're just going to head back to the main, whoops, all right, let's go back in there really quick and we're going to view the entries, all entries. When you click on the person, let's just look at this last one here, it is going to show you the exact form that they submitted and all their information and you can use this for whatever you like. If you do have payments integrated through here, which in this example we do and we haven't hooked up through PayPal, the same PayPal account that we're using for other stuff, you can, it will tell you if they purchased or not, if they used the form to make a purchase. So that is everything that you'll need to know for Wufu as far as how to view entries, how to share it, how to do a couple different things and then the rest will just be actually building out the forms that you're going to need to capture whatever information you are trying to capture. So we're going to dig into that.
